perturbation.original_text: Furthermore, the potential Franchisee agrees to not discuss or communicate any aspect whatsoever of the confidential information, be it directly or indirectly, to any agent, director, employee, or any other representative EXCEPT if these individuals (i) have been informed of the confidential nature of the information and (ii) have duly completed and signed the certificate presented in Annex “A”. The potential Franchisee will be held accountable for any violation of this Agreement committed by their directors, agents, employees, representatives, and other professional representatives.
perturbation.changed_text: Furthermore, the potential Franchisee agrees to not discuss or communicate any aspect whatsoever of the confidential information, be it directly or indirectly, to any agent, director, employee, or any other representative. The potential Franchisee will be held accountable for any violation of this Agreement committed by their directors, agents, employees, representatives, and other professional representatives.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the exception clause "EXCEPT if these individuals (i) have been informed of the confidential nature of the information and (ii) have duly completed and signed the certificate presented in Annex “A”.", section 2 becomes stricter, now forbidding any communication of confidential information to agents or employees, even if they have signed the confidentiality certificate in Annex A. This contradicts the purpose of Annex A, which is designed to allow controlled sharing of information. If it is impossible to share this information with employees that have signed annex A, this creates an internal contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Annex “A” I, undersigned, _______________________, employee or associate of the Franchise, in return for a nominal amount of 1 dollar ($ 1.00) which has been paid to me by the Franchisee, hereby recognize and confirm that I am aware of the terms and conditions of the confidentiality Agreement between ___________________, _________________, and __________________, and the Franchisor signed on ________________, 20___, and I hereby agree that I must comply with the terms and conditions presented in said Agreement. Dated this ______ day of ____________, 20___. __________________________ Employee or associate
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed the potential franchisee to share confidential information with agents, directors, and employees if they met specific conditions (informed of confidentiality and signed Annex A). The changed text removes this exception, now strictly prohibiting any communication of confidential information to these individuals. This directly contradicts Annex A, which is designed to enable controlled sharing of information and expects individuals to comply with the confidentiality agreement. Therefore, the changed text makes Annex A's purpose impossible to fulfill, creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: CONSIDERING THAT the potential Franchisee has received information—financial, operational, and other—concerning the Franchisor, including confidential, non-public information. This information has been requested by the potential Franchisee so that they may evaluate a potential transaction with the Franchisor. It is recognized that, in the event that the potential Agreement is reached, it is in the Franchisor’s best interest to make the confidential information available to the potential Franchisee so that the latter may evaluate any transaction.
perturbation.changed_text: CONSIDERING THAT the potential Franchisee has received information—financial, operational, and other—concerning the Franchisor. This information has been requested by the potential Franchisee so that they may evaluate a potential transaction with the Franchisor. It is recognized that, in the event that the potential Agreement is reached, it is in the Franchisor’s best interest to make the information available to the potential Franchisee so that the latter may evaluate any transaction.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the term "confidential, non-public information" from the first sentence, it is no longer clear what information is being shared for evaluation purposes. This creates a contradiction because section 2 discusses the need to protect confidential information, but the "Considering That" section no longer specifies it. This can cause confusion because it may not be clear whether the Franchisee needs to keep any shared information confidential since it is no longer clearly defined that it is.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Non-disclosure: The potential Franchisee recognizes that any confidential information could be used to the detriment of the Franchisor and the Franchise, and that the Franchisee disclosing any confidential information could cause irreparable damage to the Franchisor and the Franchise. The potential Franchisee hereby agrees to respect unconditionally the confidential nature of all confidential information and to never disclose or communicate any confidential information to a third party, be it directly or indirectly, or knowingly use this information for any purpose whatsoever, except with the Franchisor’s written consent. Furthermore, the potential Franchisee agrees to not discuss or communicate any aspect whatsoever of the confidential information, be it directly or indirectly, to any agent, director, employee, or any other representative EXCEPT if these individuals (i) have been informed of the confidential nature of the information and (ii) have duly completed and signed the certificate presented in Annex “A”. The potential Franchisee will be held accountable for any violation of this Agreement committed by their directors, agents, employees, representatives, and other professional representatives.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifically refers to 'confidential, non-public information,' which is then referenced in Section 2 regarding non-disclosure agreements. The changed text removes 'confidential, non-public,' making it unclear if the information shared is confidential or not. This directly contradicts Section 2, which assumes the information is confidential and requires the potential Franchisee to protect it. Because the Changed Text removes the term "confidential", it is not clearly defined that the Franchisee needs to keep any shared information confidential, therefore causing a contradiction between the Changed Text and Contradicted Text.

perturbation.original_text: CONSIDERING THAT the potential Franchisee recognizes that the Franchisor is asking them to refrain from discussing the Franchise project and from disclosing any information (be it by written, oral, or electronic channels) to a third party (as described below), even if the information is known to the general public, since discussions between the potential Franchisee and a third party could jeopardize or be detrimental to the Franchisor’s interests;
perturbation.changed_text: CONSIDERING THAT the potential Franchisee recognizes that the Franchisor is asking them to refrain from discussing the Franchise project and from disclosing any information (be it by written, oral, or electronic channels) to a third party , since discussions between the potential Franchisee and a third party could jeopardize or be detrimental to the Franchisor’s interests;
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase "(as described below), even if the information is known to the general public,", section 2 later references not disclosing any information to a third party, however by removing this phase there is no way to clarify on that, even information available to the public should not be disclosed. This creates an issue because even if public information is already available, it should still not be disclosed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Non-disclosure: The potential Franchisee recognizes that any confidential information could be used to the detriment of the Franchisor and the Franchise, and that the Franchisee disclosing any confidential information could cause irreparable damage to the Franchisor and the Franchise. The potential Franchisee hereby agrees to respect unconditionally the confidential nature of all confidential information and to never disclose or communicate any confidential information to a third party, be it directly or indirectly, or knowingly use this information for any purpose whatsoever, except with the Franchisor’s written consent. Furthermore, the potential Franchisee agrees to not discuss or communicate any aspect whatsoever of the confidential information, be it directly or indirectly, to any agent, director, employee, or any other representative EXCEPT if these individuals (i) have been informed of the confidential nature of the information and (ii) have duly completed and signed the certificate presented in Annex “A”. The potential Franchisee will be held accountable for any violation of this Agreement committed by their directors, agents, employees, representatives, and other professional representatives.
perturbation.justification: The original text included a phrase clarifying that even publicly known information should not be disclosed. The changed text removes this phrase, creating a contradiction with section 2, which broadly prohibits disclosing 'any confidential information'. The removal creates ambiguity about whether publicly available information is also considered non-disclosable, thus causing a contradiction as it removes specifics within the agreement.

perturbation.original_text: The potential Franchisee agrees that, in the event of a real or possible violation of any provision of the present Agreement by the Franchisee, the Franchisor has the right to seek an injunction in order to stop or prevent any violation of the present Agreement by the potential Franchisee, as well as the right to pursue without restriction any duty, right, or recourse legally available to the Franchisor.
perturbation.changed_text: The potential Franchisee agrees that, in the event of a real or possible violation of any provision of the present Agreement by the Franchisee, the Franchisor has the right to seek an injunction in order to stop or prevent any violation of the present Agreement by the potential Franchisee.
perturbation.explanation: Removing "as well as the right to pursue without restriction any duty, right, or recourse legally available to the Franchisor" from section 5 limits the Franchisor's remedies to only injunctions. This contradicts the implied understanding that the Franchisor would also have access to other legal remedies, such as monetary damages, that are available for breach of contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Claim for injunction: The potential Franchisee agrees that, in the event of a real or possible violation of any provision of the present Agreement by the Franchisee, the Franchisor has the right to seek an injunction in order to stop or prevent any violation of the present Agreement by the potential Franchisee.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that the Franchisor has the right to seek an injunction as well as pursue any other legally available recourse. The changed text removes 'as well as the right to pursue without restriction any duty, right, or recourse legally available to the Franchisor.' This limits the Franchisor's remedies to injunctions only, contradicting the contradicted text (which is actually a copy of the changed text) and implying the Franchisor would have access to other legal remedies, such as monetary damages, for breach of contract.
